reviewer: External chemist (user-attested) for alkaline-earth/water additions; OpenAI Codex (AI) for prior catalogue
scope: The exact generated ChemSpec catalogue containing 118 element identity records; the structures, templates, applications, premises, generalized rules, and reviewed macroscopic material records for 43 established finite bindings across alkali-metal/water (including the bounded Rb, Cs, and project-authorised Fr water-contact variants), alkaline-earth/water (Ca, Sr, and Ba with liquid water plus Mg with steam), silver-halide precipitation, strong-acid neutralization, acid-carbonate and acid-bicarbonate gas evolution, and aqueous halogen displacement; 68 representative element/oxygen structural experiences; 81 fixed-charge main-group binary ionic experiences; and 20 finite covalent-combination outcomes.
method: The user attested external-chemist review and validation of the four alkaline-earth/water additions and requested their exact presentation mappings. Codex encoded those outcomes against the cited OpenStax and Royal Society of Chemistry sources and ran the same deterministic public-schema, generalized elaboration, kernel validation, graph rewrite, electron/charge conservation, and frame-generation gates as the prior seven packages. The prior catalogue remains covered by its host-selected AI review, including exact phase/contact material records and digest binding.
coverage_conclusion: The exact catalogue is internally consistent, evidence-linked, structurally conservative, and suitable for 212 supported representative educational experiences: 43 established finite bindings, 68 oxygen reactions, 81 fixed-charge ionic pairs, and 20 finite covalent-combination outcomes. Ca, Sr, and Ba use the reviewed liquid-water hydroxide-plus-hydrogen family; magnesium is condition-distinct and uses the reviewed steam oxide-plus-hydrogen rule.
limitation: The alkaline-earth additions are recorded as externally reviewed on the user's attestation; ChemSpec does not identify or independently credential that chemist. The remainder retains an explicitly AI-reviewed trust decision and AI review can be wrong. Finite formula-unit structures and transient execution states are explanatory modeling assumptions, not physical intermediates or mechanisms. These are representative educational outcomes, not laboratory procedure, kinetics, thermodynamics, concentrations, or safety guidance.
